purpose: Trace business policies and rules into data constraints, derivations, enforcement mechanisms, and data-quality evidence.
required_context: affected terms, concepts, fact types, entities, attributes, relationships, domains, states, and events
whether the rule is advisory, mandatory, temporal, jurisdictional, or state-dependent
quality metric, threshold, owner, and remediation process
ask_if_missing: What business fact or state transition does the rule constrain?
Where must enforcement happen, and where is detection sufficient?
assume_if_low_risk: treat important nullable/not-null, unique, cardinality, domain, and derivation choices as business-rule metadata
prefer one canonical enforcement point plus observable quality checks

branches.criteria: rule can be expressed as identifier, referential, cardinality, optionality, domain, or exclusivity constraint
branches.recommendation: model it as a system constraint and map it to database and application enforcement where appropriate

branches.criteria: rule changes by lifecycle state, event, deadline, role, jurisdiction, or source
branches.recommendation: avoid a static flag; model conditional basis and enforcement timing explicitly

branches.criteria: value is derived, materialized, or cached
branches.recommendation: capture formula, source facts, effective period, recomputation trigger, source of record, and reconciliation checks
document manual control, responsible party, exception workflow, detection metric, and audit evidence

failure_modes: business rules stored only as natural-language notes
database nullability or check constraints have no business-rule trace
derived values lack source facts, recomputation triggers, or stale-value detection
enforcement duplicated across systems with no canonical owner
quality checks report technical anomalies but not business-rule violations
output_checks: links policy/rule to affected concepts and fact types
defines data-quality measurement, owner, cadence, and remediation
